candidate.title: Inference deployment-control evidence for autoscaling, placement, and rollout boundaries

repo_state.git_status_before_task: git status --short returned no changed paths before task-1 edits.

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Searched local raw/links, raw/crawler, raw/github, ai_infra manifests, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, and r7 task manifests for autoscaling, auto scaling, scale-to-zero, HPA, KEDA, Knative, KServe, Ray Serve, ServeDeployment, placement group, node selector, accelerator placement, canary, rollout, rollback, traffic split, postmortem, incident, SLO, trace, and profiler terms. Existing sources covered vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, Triton, llama.cpp, ONNX Runtime GenAI, PegaFlow external KV-cache operations, vLLM RL rollout weight-sync, SGLang router abort observability, PD disaggregation failure handling, KubeRay scheduling/autoscaling mentions, and general alerting mechanics, but no bounded inference deployment-control source note existed for Ray Serve autoscaling/placement or revision traffic-split rollout mechanics.
local_checks.wiki_search: Inference Runtime Infrastructure and AI Infra Coverage Map explicitly listed autoscaling, model-placement, canary rollout, full postmortems, live trace/SLO artifacts, and optional SGLang API refresh as remaining inference-runtime gaps. Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure mentioned KubeRay autoscaling and label scheduling only as Ray/Kubernetes workload-controller evidence, not Ray Serve deployment controls. Evaluation Observability Reliability Infrastructure covered alert/SLO mechanics but not serving rollout or placement behavior.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md already routes Inference Runtime Infrastructure, AI Infra Coverage Map, Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure, and Evaluation Observability Reliability Infrastructure; this task updates existing reference pages and does not create a standalone page.

duplicate_checks.result: primary duplicate boundary for serving runtime mechanics and r7 operations evidence; updated only with deployment-control evidence for autoscaling, placement, and rollout boundaries.
duplicate boundary for KubeRay, Slurm, device plugins, GPU operators, Kueue, and Volcano scheduling evidence; not edited because Ray Serve deployment controls belong in inference-runtime.
duplicate boundary for trace, profiling, SLO, alerting, and platform observability mechanics; not edited because this task does not add live trace/SLO artifacts.

duplicate boundary for PegaFlow, vLLM rollout weight-sync, SGLang router abort observability, PD disaggregation, startup/cache/benchmark leads, and local inference operations; task-1 avoids repeating those claims.

selected_source_candidates.coverage: Ray Serve autoscaling config with min/max replicas and target ongoing requests; Serve resource allocation for CPUs, GPUs, custom resources, and accelerator type; placement-group/gang scheduling for multi-actor replicas; Serve-controlled in-place or rolling deployment updates.

Knative Serving request-load autoscaling, inactive revision scale-down, immutable revisions, percentage traffic splits, latest-revision rollout, blue/green staging, and rollback-by-traffic-shift boundaries.

gap_reason: The local wiki and raw corpus already covered inference runtime mechanics, vLLM/PegaFlow operations, SGLang issue-level operational leads, KubeRay workload-controller scheduling, and alerting mechanics, but did not preserve bounded model-serving deployment-control evidence for request-driven autoscaling, accelerator placement inputs, gang placement of multi-actor serving replicas, or revision traffic-split rollout/rollback mechanics. The selected Ray Serve and Knative sources fill those control-plane surfaces while leaving KServe-specific behavior, complete production postmortems, live trace/SLO artifacts, and full SGLang API refresh evidence open.
